subject: [PATCH v7 2/2] NFSD: convert callback RPC program to per-net namespace

reviews.author: Chuck Lever
reviews.summary: Reviewer Chuck Lever noted that embedding the nfsd_cb struct inside nfsd_net would create a tight coupling between netns.h and the NFSv4 callback program, which he avoided by using a pointer to hide implementation details.
reviews.raw_body:
The reason I did this is because otherwise netns.h would need
to have knowledge of the details of the NFSv4 callback program.
Things like the NFSv4 callback program's RPC program number
are implementation-defined...

I'm avoiding a weak layering violation by hiding those details
behind a pointer.


-- 
Chuck Lever

reviews.author: Jeff Layton (author)
reviews.summary: Author acknowledges that embedding nfsd_cb inside nfsd_net would have been a better approach, but considers it a minor issue and does not plan to restructure the code in this patch version.
reviews.raw_body:
Meh. I'd probably have prioritized avoiding the extra allocation, but
that's a minor thing.

Reviewed-by: Jeff Layton <jlayton@kernel.org>

2026-03-14.patch_summary: This patch simplifies the NFSD callback RPC program by embedding its struct directly within the nfsd_net structure, eliminating the need for a separate allocation.
